strategic_profile.executive_summary: Adelphi University is a private, mid-sized research institution in suburban Long Island noted for its personalized 11:1 student-to-faculty ratio and proximity to New York City. The university excels in pre-professional training, boasting highly-ranked programs in Nursing, Psychology, and Business.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The College of Nursing and Public Health ranks in the top 28% nationally and utilizes a rigorous direct-admit or pre-nursing pathway.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants must prepare for intense GPA cutoffs and strict science prerequisites to survive the 'weed-out' phases of healthcare majors.

analyst_takeaways.insight: 92% of students receive institutional aid, bringing the average net price down to $31,303 despite a $73,000+ COA.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Generous merit and need-based discounting makes Adelphi financially viable despite high nominal tuition costs.

analyst_takeaways.insight: A hybrid residential and commuter campus located 40 minutes from NYC.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students benefit from tremendous metropolitan internship access but must actively engage in campus clubs to navigate the commuter-heavy weekend culture.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Includes over 90 clubs, Greek life, and Division II NCAA athletics. Known as a supportive, inclusive environment where students receive individualized attention.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Suburban campus with direct, easy train access to New York City, serving heavily as both a commuter hub and residential college.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Strong focus on faculty-led undergraduate research, particularly in clinical psychology, artificial intelligence in business, and public health.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional for general admission; SAT/ACT scores are still required for Joint Degree and Early Assurance programs.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist data not publicly disclosed. Admissions office confirms applicants on the waitlist are unranked.

gpa.notes: Recommended GPA target is 2.95 or higher, though competitive programs like Nursing demand a 3.20+.

testing.policy_note: Test-optional; non-submitters remain fully eligible for robust merit scholarships.

colleges.name: College of Nursing and Public Health
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students with highly rigorous high school science backgrounds seeking careers in clinical care.

majors.name: Nursing
majors.special_requirements: High school direct admits need a 3.20+ GPA. Transfers require a 3.0 GPA, TEAS Version V exam, and no more than one science prerequisite below a C+.
majors.notes: Students admitted as 'Pre-Nursing' must maintain a 3.0 GPA and earn no less than a C+ in their freshman year science courses to advance into the major.

colleges.name: Robert B. Willumstad School of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Career-minded students interested in quantitative fields, AI integration, management, and corporate networking.
colleges.student_archetype: Driven corporate aspirants and future entrepreneurs.

majors.name: Business Administration
notable_professors: Dr. Yifan Xie (Decision Sciences and Marketing)
majors.notes: The curriculum actively integrates artificial intelligence and machine learning into core business instruction.

supplemental_requirements.details: Requires a personal statement detailing interest in the program.
Optional essay to share interest in the social work profession.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Interviews and Test Scores are explicitly required for Joint Degree and Early Assurance pre-med/pre-dental programs.

major_selection_tactics: Select 'Pre-Nursing' if your GPA is close to, but under, the 3.20 cutoff for Direct-Admit Nursing.
Withhold test scores unless they are cleanly above 1120 to guarantee optimal merit aid processing.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Pre-Nursing acts as the primary alternate major. If a student fails the freshman weed-out requirements, internal transfer to Social Work or allied health is a common backup plan.

financials.tuition_model: Flat rate for full-time undergraduate students.

ap_policy.usage: Maximum of 30 credits accepted combined between AP and IB.

ib_policy.general_rule: Grants credit for qualified Higher Level (HL) courses.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Maximum 90 credits accepted. Standard transfer requires a C- or better; Nursing science prerequisites require a C+ or better and must be completed within 10 years.

what_it_takes: Clear professional goals, particularly for nursing and business applicants
red_flags: Poor performance in foundational high school sciences (for nursing)
Applying to health programs without laboratory science experience
insights: The campus is highly commuter-centric, though residential dorms exist.
Anatomy & Physiology acts as a severe weed-out course for pre-nursing students.
Professors are extremely accessible due to the 11:1 ratio.
